food_subgroup: Packaged sliced white bread — commercial, NOVA Class 3

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for packaged commercial white bread; FoodOn and FoodEx2 anchors are sufficient.

serving.natural_language_portion: Two standard slices of Gardenia Classic White Bread, approximately 60g, representing the typical Singapore breakfast toast portion used for kaya toast or peanut butter toast.

nutrient_source.title: Gardenia Classic White Bread — Gardenia Singapore Product Label (Nutrition Information Panel)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Gardenia Classic White Bread product label (Nutrition Information Panel) as sold in Singapore. Values are per 100g. Secondary cross-reference: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for white bread (Item 18069) used to validate micronutrient plausibility. All values consistent with label data.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — commercial white bread, soft, fully gelatinised
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 70 sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for commercial soft white sliced bread (fully gelatinised). Gardenia Classic White Bread is a commercial soft white sliced bread of equivalent formulation. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (70 × 30.24) / 100 = 21.2, rounded to 20.2 for a 60g serving (30.24g available carbohydrate). Note: input record cited GL of 33.6 for 100g reference portion; per 60g serving GL = 21.2 is used here.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content ~503mg per 100g (2 standard slices ~60g = ~302mg sodium)
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should be aware that two slices of Gardenia Classic White Bread contribute approximately 300mg sodium — about 13–15% of the 2,000mg daily sodium target — making bread a significant hidden sodium source when consumed multiple times daily; those eating 3–4 slices per day should monitor total daily sodium intake carefully.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 70) and low dietary fibre (2g per 100g) producing rapid postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Gardenia Classic White Bread has a GI of 70 (high) and provides only 2g dietary fibre per 100g, resulting in a glycemic load of approximately 21 per standard 2-slice serving; patients with Type 2 Diabetes are strongly advised to switch to Gardenia Wholemeal or Hi-Fibre bread (GI ~58, fibre ~6g per 100g), which reduces glycemic load by approximately 24% with no change in meal format.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Refined carbohydrate dietary pattern, moderate sodium, low saturated fat (0.8g per 100g), zero trans fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While Gardenia Classic White Bread is low in saturated fat (0.8g per 100g) and contains zero trans fat, its high refined carbohydrate content (GI 70, 50g carbohydrate per 100g) and moderate sodium (503mg per 100g) are consistent with dietary patterns associated with elevated cardiovascular risk; patients with CVD should limit daily intake and prefer wholegrain alternatives to reduce refined carbohydrate load and support better lipid and glycaemic profiles.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High-GI refined carbohydrate associated with pro-inflammatory cytokine patterns
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: High-GI refined white bread is associated with elevated postprandial insulin and glucose spikes linked to pro-inflammatory cytokine patterns; patients with rheumatoid arthritis following an anti-inflammatory dietary approach should prefer wholegrain bread alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Gardenia Classic White Bread is the dominant packaged sliced white bread brand in Singapore, produced by Gardenia Bakeries (Singapore) Pte Ltd since 1978. It is a soft, fully gelatinised commercial white bread made from refined white wheat flour, water, sugar, yeast, salt, vegetable shortening, and bread improver. The bread is baked and sold pre-sliced in standard loaves. Texture is soft and pillowy with a thin crust; flavour is mild and slightly sweet. It is classified as NOVA Class 3 (processed food). Per 100g (approximately 2 standard slices of ~50g each): 269 kcal, 8g protein, 3g fat (0.8g saturated, 0g trans), 50.4g carbohydrate, 4.3g sugar, 2g dietary fibre, 503mg sodium. Glycemic Index: 70 (HIGH). Glycemic Load per 60g serving (2 slices): approximately 21. This is one of the highest-GI staple foods in the Singapore diet. The very low fibre content (2g per 100g) provides no meaningful moderation of the glucose response. Gardenia white bread is the standard bread for Singapore kaya toast (with kaya jam and butter), peanut butter toast, and sandwich lunches. It is halal-certified, vegan, and dairy-free. Clinical note: For patients with Type 2 Diabetes, switching from Gardenia Classic White to Gardenia Wholemeal or Hi-Fibre bread (GI ~58, fibre ~6g) is one of the highest-impact, lowest-disruption dietary modifications available in the Singapore context — reducing glycemic load by ~24% with no change in meal format. Sodium content (~503mg per 100g) makes bread a significant hidden sodium contributor for hypertensive patients eating multiple slices daily.
common_user_queries: gardenia classic white bread nutrition facts
how many calories in 2 slices of white bread singapore
is white bread bad for diabetes singapore

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Gardenia Classic White Bread product label (Nutrition Information Panel) as sold in Singapore — an exact product match. GI value of 70 from the University of Sydney GI Database for commercial soft white sliced bread, which is the appropriate analogous reference for this product type. All core macros and secondary nutrients are complete and consistent with the product label and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy white bread reference.
notes: trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per Gardenia Singapore product label. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per product label — white bread contains no animal-derived cholesterol-bearing ingredients. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_d_mcg, and vitamin_b12_mcg are confirmed zero per product label; refined white bread is not a source of these vitamins. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported on the Gardenia Singapore product label and not available in the Singapore HPB database for this specific product; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy white bread (Item 18069) reports MUFA ~0.4g and PUFA ~1.4g per 100g as a plausible reference but these are not confirmed for this specific Gardenia formulation and are therefore not populated. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported on the Gardenia Singapore product label. Glycemic load per serving calculated for a 60g serving (2 standard slices of ~30g each per Gardenia/Sunshine standard): GL = (70 × 30.24g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 21.2. The input record cited GL of 33.6 for the full 100g reference portion; the serving-based GL of ~21 is used in this record as the serving size is 60g. Two portions were listed in the input (70g and 60g); 60g is adopted as the default serving size per the Gardenia standard slice weight of ~30g per slice. Country of origin: Singapore. NOVA classification: Class 3 (processed food). Halal-certified by MUIS (Majlis Ugama Islam Singapura).
